recommendation.title: Bull Call Spread — Post Q1 Beat; NVIDIA Equity Stake Is the Strongest Bull Signal
recommendation.entryNote: Q1 2026 reported April 30 (+4.2%) — IV crush from Q1 has passed. Next earnings likely July/August 2026 — clean entry window of 90+ days. HIGH IV (~55–70%) from volatile AI infrastructure sector mandates spread. Goldman Sachs PT $205 is the bull case. Most asymmetric setup on this list: $23 → $155 in 12 months (+567%); contracts with Microsoft ($19.4B) and Meta ($27B) total $46B in backlog; NVIDIA invested $2B equity directly — the most credible institutional validation possible. KEY RISK: $14.7M insider selling in 90 days (Director + CRO + CIO). Some are 10b5-1 plans but the volume is notable. Dilution is structural: 40% of funding still unraised, $4.6B convertible notes, ATM equity program active. Size to 1% portfolio max due to dilution. 1 spread at $1,000–1,400 is slightly over budget — enter 1 only.

exitPlan.label: Spread 1
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $170–175 (+10–13%) on ARR milestone or new contract announcement

exitPlan.label: Spread 2
exitPlan.trigger: Stock at $185+ (+19%) on Goldman $205 target chase or next quarterly beat

companyOverview: Nebius Group N.V. is an Amsterdam-headquartered AI infrastructure company that emerged from the international (non-Russian) assets of Yandex following the sale of Yandex's Russian operations in July 2024 due to geopolitical sanctions. The remaining assets — a Finnish data centre, the Nebius AI cloud unit, and related European/Israeli tech businesses — were rebranded as Nebius Group and resumed NASDAQ trading in October 2024 under ticker NBIS. The company has since signed extraordinary contracts: a $19.4B five-year agreement with Microsoft (September 2025), subsequently expanded with Meta to a total of $27B (March 2026). NVIDIA announced a $2B direct equity investment in Nebius as a strategic partner for next-generation hyperscale AI infrastructure — the strongest possible third-party validation. 2026 revenue guidance: $3–$3.4B; 2026 CapEx: $16–$20B (spending $5+ for every $1 of revenue to build out infrastructure). The stock has delivered 567% returns from its 52-week low ($23.25) to current levels (~$155).

financials.note: Q1 reported April 30, 2026 — beat expectations; positive market reaction
Aggressive growth; CapEx $16–20B = massive infrastructure investment
NVIDIA invested $2B directly — strategic validator of NBIS technology
Avg PT: $169; Goldman Sachs PT $205; range $143–$211
Elevated for a mid-cap AI infrastructure name — spread recommended

insiderFlow.summary: Net SELLING — $14.7M in 90 days across Director, CRO, CIO, and CEO (tax withholding). Mixed: some planned 10b5-1 sales, some open-market. Yellow flag but not as severe as CRWV — volume is smaller relative to company size and institutional validators (NVIDIA, Goldman) offset.

transactions.note: ⚠️ Open-market sales under 10b5-1 plan. Sold progressively higher prices ($140→$161) — pre-planned but notable volume for a director.
10b5-1 plan. Selling at $160 = near 52-week high. Pre-planned.
⚠️ Largest single insider sale: 31,357 shares at $111 (open market). This was before the recent run to $155 — sold at a discount to current price.
Automatic tax withholding upon RSU vesting. Not discretionary selling — most benign category.

insiderFlow.signal: YELLOW — $14.7M selling in 90 days. CIO sold 31K shares open-market. Director sold progressively. BUT: CEO sales were tax withholding (not discretionary), NVIDIA's $2B equity stake from outside validates management credibility. Net: yellow flag, not red.

catalysts: NVIDIA $2B direct equity investment — the most credible external validation possible; NVIDIA doesn't invest $2B without deep diligence
Microsoft $19.4B contract + Meta $27B expansion = $46B total backlog — multi-year revenue visibility that dwarfs current valuation
Q1 2026 beat (+4.2% stock reaction April 30) — fundamental momentum confirmed; next report clean entry window
Goldman Sachs PT $205 — upgraded with 'boosted forecasts from major contracts'; 32% upside from current price
Yandex provenance = world-class AI/ML engineering team (Yandex was Russia's Google); tech DNA is genuinely differentiated
NVIDIA GB200 NVL72 rack allocation: as NVIDIA's strategic partner, NBIS likely gets preferred GPU access — competitive moat
risks: DILUTION IS STRUCTURAL: $16–20B CapEx in 2026 against $3–3.4B revenue means the company is spending 5x+ what it earns. 40% of this funding is UNRAISED. Expect material equity issuance in 2026.
$4.6B convertible notes already issued — conversion will dilute existing shareholders; ATM equity program further dilutes
$14.7M insider selling in 90 days: CIO's 31K open-market sale and Director's progressive sales are yellow flags
Yandex origin: despite asset separation, any geopolitical escalation or new sanctions allegations could impact stock (headline risk, not fundamental)
High IV (~55–70%): options are expensive; enter via spread to contain cost
Revenue/CapEx ratio (1:5+): company is massively pre-investing; if hyperscaler demand slows, the economics of $20B CapEx become unsustainable
52-week high $168.71 is nearby resistance — stock has made a 567% run and distribution risk exists near ATH

dilution.summary: HIGH DILUTION RISK — structural and ongoing. 40% of $16–20B CapEx is unraised. Convertible notes + ATM equity program already in place. Expect share count to grow materially in 2026.

items.label: $4.6B convertible notes (issued)
items.detail: Convert to equity at maturity or conversion triggers. Direct dilution built into the capital structure.

items.label: ATM equity program (active)
items.detail: Allows management to issue new shares at market price on an ongoing basis. Provides maximum dilution flexibility — and maximum dilution risk.

items.label: 40% of 2026 CapEx unraised
items.detail: Of the $16–20B 2026 CapEx, only 60% is committed. The remainder needs debt, asset-backed financing, or equity. Size positions accordingly.

items.label: NVIDIA's $2B equity stake
items.detail: This is NEW equity issued to NVIDIA — already accretive to dilution count but validates the business plan.
